Editor The Press: Life for the
common people In modern Ameri-
ca today Is like q Chinese puzzle
you never know where you are at.
Today all Of our large cities are
owned and absolutely controlled by
the railroads that traverse them


For instance Spokane, where Jim
Hill holds first mortgage and the
Washington Water Power Co. takes
care of the second mortgage. AT
present we have numerous big
hearted candidates telling the peo-
pie how prosperous they are, and
how much more success will be
theirs If they are elected-a close
investigation reveals big heart,
but so tough that you could not
drive a railroad spike in 1t. They
are all so interested In the dear
common people just previous to
election, and get on to that jackass
smile when they extend their mitt
